perturbation.original_text: This Agreement will be interpreted in accordance with the laws of the France . Should a dispute arise concerning the interpretation, validity or execution of this agreement, the Parties shall bring the disput e to French Courts, which shall exclusively be competent.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement will be interpreted in accordance with the laws of France. Should a dispute arise concerning the interpretation, validity or execution of this agreement, the Parties agree to binding arbitration in Paris, France, under rules determined by Inria.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the dispute resolution from French Courts to binding arbitration introduces a contradiction. While arbitration is a valid method, specifying 'rules determined by Inria' introduces bias and conflicts with standard legal practices for fair dispute resolution. This deviates from the expected legal framework.
perturbation.contradicted_law: French Code of Civil Procedure, Articles 1442 et seq.
perturbation.law_explanation: French law outlines specific requirements for arbitration agreements, including impartiality and fairness. Granting Inria unilateral control over arbitration rules contradicts these requirements, potentially rendering the arbitration clause unenforceable under French law.

giant" quote Tomb Retour des cendres Penis Studies v t e The Napoleonic Code ( French : Code Napoléon ), officially the Civil Code of the French ( French : Code civil des Français ; simply referred to as Code civil ), is the French civil code established during the French Consulate in 1804 and still in force in France, although heavily and frequently amended since its inception. [ 1 ] Although Napoleon himself was not directly involved in the drafting of the Code, as it was drafted by a commission of four eminent jurists, [ 2 ] he chaired many of the commission's plenary sessions, [ 3 ] and his support was crucial to its enactment. perturbation.justification: The original contract specifies dispute resolution in French Courts. The changed text replaces this with binding arbitration under rules determined by Inria. The Law Explanation states that French law requires impartiality and fairness in arbitration agreements and that granting Inria unilateral control over arbitration rules contradicts these requirements. The change creates a contradiction because it potentially undermines the impartiality required by French law for arbitration, as Inria's rules may not be neutral, potentially rendering the arbitration clause unenforceable under French law. While the provided scraped text does not directly show this, the Law Explanation provides enough legal reasoning to support the contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The GTL Data remain the exclusive so le property of Inria. The present Agreement does not grant any transfer or assignment of commercial/exploitation/property rights on identified know -how, patents, software or any other intellectual property right held by Inria to the Organization.
perturbation.changed_text: The GTL Data remain the exclusive property of Inria, except where the Organization creates novel intellectual property using said data. In such a case, the Organization retains ownership of the new IP, with a perpetual, royalty-free license granted back to Inria for internal research purposes only.
perturbation.explanation: The original text clearly states Inria retains all IP rights. The modified text introduces an exception where the Organization creates new IP. This creates ambiguity and potential conflict over IP ownership, which is governed by French IP law.
perturbation.contradicted_law: French Intellectual Property Code (Code de la propriété intellectuelle), Articles L111-1 et seq.
perturbation.law_explanation: French IP law dictates how ownership of intellectual property is established and transferred. The modified text creates a situation where the ownership of derived IP is ambiguously defined, potentially conflicting with the established legal framework for IP rights in France.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: nce for Overland Trade ( Code Marchand ), and the 1681 Ordinance for Maritime Trade ( Code de la Marine ). [ 32 ] Ordinances would later be drawn up on Donations (1731), Wills (1735), Falsifications (1737), and Trustees (1747), but a unified code of private law would not be passed until 1804, under Napoleon and after the French Revolution . [ 32 ] Under King Louis XV , [ 36 ] there would be a constant struggle between royal legislation, traditional conceptions of the law of the Realm (customs and Roman law), and parliamentary arrêts de règlements (regulatory decisions). [ 37 ] [ 32 ] Judges sided with the local parliaments (judicial bodies in France) and the landed aristocracy, undermining royal authority and legislation. [ 38 ] [ 39 ] Even before the French Revolution, French enlightenment thinkers like Jean-Jacques Rousseau , with a theory of natural rights, and especially Montesquieu , who advocated for a separation of powers, were major influences on the law throughout Europe and the United States. [ 40 ] [ 32 ] The French legal system underwent great changes after the French Revolution beginning in 1789, which swept away the old regime . [ 32 ] By 1790, the National Constituent Assembly overhauled the country’s judicial system. [ 32 ] A Penal Code would be adopted by 1791. The Civil Code (1804), the Code of Civil Procedure (1806), and the Commercial Code (1807) were adopted under Napoleon , reflecting Roman law, pre-revolutionary ordinances and custom, scholarly legal writings, enlightenment ideas, and Napoleon's personal vision of the law. [ 32 ] These codes consisted of numbered articles, were written in elegant French, and were meant to be understood by the layman. [ 28 ] [ 5 ] In addition, they introduced many classically liberal reforms, such as abolishing remaining feudal institutions and establishing rights of personality, property and contract for all male French citizens. [ 41 ] However, not all the old regime's law were repleted, the articles 110 and 111 of the 1539 Ordinance of Villers-Cotterêts being the oldest still in use in the French legislation. Private law [ edit ] This section does not cite any sources . Please help improve this section by adding citations to reliable sources . Unsourced material may be challenged and removed . ( February 2023 ) ( Learn how and when to remove this message ) This section may need to be rewritten to comply with Wikipedia's quality standards . You can help . The talk page may contain suggestions. ( February 2023 ) The term civil law in France refers to private law (laws between private citizens, and should be distinguished from the group of legal systems descended from Roman Law known as civil law , as opposed to common law . The major private law codes include: The Civil Code , The Code of Civil Procedure , The Commercial Code, and The Intellectual Property Code. Civil procedure [ edit ] France follows an inquisitorial model , where the judge leads the proceedings and the gathering of evidence, acting in the public interest to bring out the truth of a case. [ 42 ] This is contrasted with the adversarial model often seen in common law countries, where parties in the case play a primary role in the judicial process. [ 42 ] In French civil cases, one party has the burden of proof, according to law, but both sides and the judge together gather and provide evidence. [ 42 ] There is no strict standard of proof in civil cases, like the preponderance of the evidence under American law; instead, primacy is given to the judge's intime conviction, [ 42 ] based on the principle of "free evaluation of the evidence." [ 43 ] The court gathers a dossier of pleadings, statements of fact and evidence from the parties and makes it available to them. [ 42 ] [ 44 ] Proceedings focus on written evidence and written argument, with brief hearings. [ 42 ] Witness testimonies are uncommon. [ 42 ] The ministère public , an independent judicial official, sometimes plays an advisory role in civil procee
perturbation.justification: The original text clearly states Inria retains all IP rights. The modified text introduces an exception where the Organization creates new IP. This creates ambiguity and potential conflict over IP ownership. The Law Explanation states that the modified text creates a situation where the ownership of derived IP is ambiguously defined, potentially conflicting with the established legal framework for IP rights in France. While the scraped snippets don't directly address IP ownership, the Law Explanation's description of French IP law, coupled with the changed text's creation of an exception to IP ownership creates a Misaligned Terminology Legal Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Promptly upon expiration or termination of this Agreement, user will cease using all GTL Data, and will return to Inria, or destroy, all originals and all copies of the GTL Data and other materials provided by Inria under this Agreement, and also certify such return or destruction in a reasonable and customary certificate provided by Inria.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon expiration or termination of this Agreement, the user will cease using all GTL Data and destroy all originals and copies, except for any data incorporated into published research, which may be retained for archival purposes indefinitely, so long as the data is not used for commercial purposes.
perturbation.explanation: The original text requires complete cessation of use and return/destruction of data. The modified text allows retention of data incorporated into published research. This creates a loophole that conflicts with the initial requirement of complete data cessation, introducing uncertainty in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_law: French Law on Confidence (Loi relative à la confiance dans l'économie numérique), Article 6
perturbation.law_explanation: French law emphasizes data security and control, especially regarding confidential information. Allowing indefinite retention of GTL data, even if incorporated into research, potentially contradicts the initial agreement's intent to maintain confidentiality after termination, raising concerns under data protection regulations.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: Article VI | U.S. Constitution | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you Article VI Primary tabs All debts contracted and engagements entered into, before the adoption of this Constitution, shall be as valid against the United States under this Constitution, as under the Confederation. This Constitution, and the laws of the United States which shall be made in pursuance thereof; and all treaties made, or which shall be made, under the authority of the United States, shall be the supreme law of the land; and the judges in every state shall be bound thereby, anything in the Constitution or laws of any State to the contrary notwithstanding. The Senators and Representatives before mentioned, and the members of the several state legislatures, and all executive and judicial officers, both of the United States and of the several states, shall be bound by oath or affirmation, to support this Constitution; but no religious test shall ever be required as a qualification to any office or public trust under the United States. ‹ Article V up Article VII › U.S. Constitution Toolbox Explanation of the Constitution - from the Congressional Research Service
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates complete cessation of GTL data use and return/destruction upon agreement termination. The changed text introduces an exception, permitting indefinite retention of data incorporated into published research, provided it's not for commercial purposes. This exception directly contradicts the initial requirement of complete data cessation, creating a legal contradiction as it undermines the original agreement's intended data control. The perturbation introduces uncertainty in enforcement, aligning with the 'Misaligned Terminology - Legal Contradiction' definition where contractual language deviates from clear obligations, potentially making the contract unenforceable as the term 'incorporated into published research' introduces ambiguity. While the scraped law snippets are not directly relevant, the core of the contradiction lies in the direct conflict between the original and modified terms regarding data handling post-termination. The changed text's introduction of a loophole, regardless of commercial use, undermines the original agreement's intention.

perturbation.original_text: Inria may terminate this Agreement, or suspend the delivery of data hereunder, for cause at any time if Organization breaches this Agreement or without cause at an y time for whatever reason.
perturbation.changed_text: Inria may terminate this Agreement, or suspend the delivery of data hereunder, at any time for any reason, provided that the Organization is given 24 hours written notice. Inria is not responsible for any damages arising from said termination, regardless of cause.
perturbation.explanation: The original text allows termination for cause or without cause. The modified text adds a 24-hour notice provision but also disclaims liability for any damages arising from termination. This creates a conflict, as French law may imply certain obligations or liabilities even with a 'no cause' termination, especially if it causes undue hardship or harm.
perturbation.law_explanation: French Civil Code Article 1104 requires parties to perform contracts in good faith. While the contract permits termination, a complete disclaimer of liability, regardless of the reason, could be seen as a violation of this 'good faith' requirement, especially if termination occurs maliciously or causes significant, avoidable damage to the Organization. This raises questions about the enforceability of the liability disclaimer.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 2-104. Definitions: "Merchant"; "Between Merchants"; "Financing Agency". | Uniform Commercial Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you § 2-104. Definitions: "Merchant"; "Between Merchants"; "Financing Agency". (1) " Merchant " means a person who deals in goods of the kind or otherwise by his occupation holds himself out as having knowledge or skill peculiar to the practices or goods involved in the transaction or to whom such knowledge or skill may be attributed by his employment of an agent or broker or other intermediary who by his occupation holds himself out as having such knowledge or skill. (2) " Financing agency " means a bank, finance company or other person who in the ordinary course of business makes advances against goods or documents of title or who by arrangement with either the seller or the buyer intervenes in ordinary course to make or collect payment due or claimed under the contract for sale , as by purchasing or paying the seller's draft or making advances against it or by merely taking it for collection whether or not documents of title accompany the draft. "Financing agency" includes also a bank or other person who similarly intervenes between persons who are in the position of seller and buyer in respect to the goods (Section 2-707 ). (3) " Between Merchants " means in any transaction with respect to which both parties are chargeable with the knowledge or skill of merchants . Uniform Commercial Code Toolbox About Uniform Laws State Uniform Commercial Codes
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces a contradiction because it disclaims liability for any damages arising from termination, regardless of cause, potentially conflicting with the requirement to perform contracts in good faith under French Civil Code Article 1104, as explained in the Law Explanation. While Scraped Law Snippet 1 is not directly relevant, the Perturbation and Law Explanations highlight the conflict between the disclaimer of liability and the good faith requirement. This misalignment of terminology, where 'termination for any reason' is coupled with a full liability waiver, creates a legal contradiction because it could allow Inria to terminate the agreement in bad faith, causing significant damage to the Organization without recourse.
